landing.subtitle: Ten minutes. One thought.
landing.howItWorksText: Take a thought that’s been nagging you. Klattur walks you through it step by step,  audio-guided, at your own pace. You examine what that thought does to you. And most importantly, what’s left once you’ve checked it.
landing.scienceBackedText: Clinical research with nearly 200 participants found that people using this approach felt significantly less anxious, less low, and less angry and that this effect was still measurable six months later. A second study with a control group showed a significant reduction in burnout symptoms among those who followed the method. In short: it works. And many people notice a difference after just one session.
landing.studyText: Stress about a test, an assignment, or the feeling that you just can’t keep up.
landing.relationsText: Something bothering you about your partner, a friend, your parents, or an ex.
landing.workText: A thought about your manager, a colleague, or how you’re performing.
landing.yourSelfText: A belief about who you are, how you look, or what you can or can’t do.
landing.somethingElseText: Doesn’t fit any of the above? Just start.
landing.extraText: Pick what’s playing right now. Do a session. See what’s left of that thought afterwards.

start.instructions: Take your time. Write down your answers or remember them, whatever works for you. And while you listen: notice what happens inside. Sometimes your body knows more than your head.

credits.thought: The thought inquiry in Klattur is based on The Work by Byron Katie.
credits.theWork: Byron Katie has made The Work freely available for everyone to use.
credits.official: Klattur is not officially affiliated with Byron Katie International.

privacy.feedback: When you give feedback or get in touch:
privacy.name: - Name and email (optional)
privacy.use: - We use this to respond and to keep building Klattur
privacy.anonym: You can use Klattur fully anonymously.
